authRequired.title: Login Required
authRequired.subtitle: Please log in to access our financial tools and calculators.

header.title: Financial Tools
header.subtitle: Each tool answers one clear question and links to the next step.

portfolio.title: Portfolio Analyzer
portfolio.promise: See if your portfolio matches your goals and risk comfort.
portfolio.description: Analyze and optimize your investment portfolio
portfolio.mostUsefulFor: Anyone already investing or thinking about reallocating.
portfolio.whatItDoes: Breaks down holdings, performance, and allocation in one view.
portfolio.sampleUseCase: Check if your portfolio is too concentrated in one asset.
portfolio.whoItsFor: Investors who want clarity on risk, concentration, and balance.
portfolio.questionItAnswers: Is my portfolio aligned with my goals and risk comfort?

reality-check.title: Savings & Goals Reality Check
reality-check.promise: Get a realistic saving range without false precision.
reality-check.description: See if your goals are realistic for your income and costs
reality-check.mostUsefulFor: People with a goal and uncertain monthly cash flow.
reality-check.whatItDoes: Turns a goal into a realistic saving range and feasibility check.
reality-check.sampleUseCase: Plan a $6k emergency fund in 12 months without guesswork.
reality-check.whoItsFor: Anyone who wants an honest view of what's achievable.
reality-check.questionItAnswers: Can I actually hit this goal with my current cash flow?

calendar.title: Economic Calendar
calendar.promise: Understand how big economic events affect your money.
calendar.description: Track macro events with context, not just dates
calendar.mostUsefulFor: Anyone trying to make sense of inflation and rates.
calendar.whatItDoes: Shows key economic events and explains why they matter.
calendar.sampleUseCase: See how CPI or rate decisions could affect savings rates.
calendar.whoItsFor: Anyone trying to understand the bigger picture.
calendar.questionItAnswers: What's happening in the economy and why should I care?

economic-map.title: Economic Map
economic-map.promise: See macro changes around the world in one glance.
economic-map.description: Live global economic data visualized on a map
economic-map.whatItDoes: Displays economic indicators on a world map.
economic-map.sampleUseCase: Check regional trends before reading the news.
economic-map.whoItsFor: People who want a global macro view.
economic-map.questionItAnswers: Where are economic shifts happening right now?

news-context.title: News & Market Context
news-context.promise: Turn headlines into simple, personal takeaways.
news-context.description: Curated finance news with plain-English meaning
news-context.mostUsefulFor: People who want news without panic or hype.
news-context.whatItDoes: Summarizes what headlines mean for everyday money decisions.
news-context.sampleUseCase: See how a rate cut impacts loans or savings.
news-context.whoItsFor: People who want news without the noise.
news-context.questionItAnswers: What does this headline mean for me?

market-explorer.title: Market Explorer
market-explorer.promise: Scan major markets with simple, long-term framing.
market-explorer.description: Explore stocks, ETFs, crypto, and indices in one view
market-explorer.mostUsefulFor: Curious learners exploring markets for the first time.
market-explorer.whatItDoes: Lets you explore markets while surfacing volatility warnings.
market-explorer.sampleUseCase: Compare S&P 500 vs bonds before making a decision.
market-explorer.whoItsFor: Learners deciding what to explore next.
market-explorer.questionItAnswers: What markets should I look at, and with what caution?

next-steps.title: What Should I Learn Next?
next-steps.promise: Get 1-3 focused learning suggestions for your next step.
next-steps.description: Personalized learning suggestions based on your progress
next-steps.mostUsefulFor: Anyone who wants a clear learning next move.
next-steps.whatItDoes: Suggests 1-3 focused learning actions based on your activity.
next-steps.sampleUseCase: Get a short list of learning and tool steps that matter.
next-steps.whoItsFor: Anyone who wants clear, prioritized learning next steps.
next-steps.questionItAnswers: What should I learn next right now?

realityCheck.description: Define the goal, then sanity-check your monthly reality.
realityCheck.emptyState: Start by entering a goal amount and timeframe to see a realistic range.
realityCheck.warningLowSurplus: Even your best-month surplus looks too low for this timeline.
realityCheck.warningReduceExpenses: You'll likely need to reduce expenses or extend the timeline.
realityCheck.warningExpensesHigher: Expenses may be higher than income in a typical month.
realityCheck.leverIncome: Increase monthly income or extend the timeline
realityCheck.leverExpenses: Reduce monthly expenses by a small amount
realityCheck.leverFixedCosts: Lower fixed costs to regain breathing room

portfolio.title: Portfolio Analyzer
portfolio.subtitle: Track and analyze your investment portfolio
portfolio.noEntriesSubtitle: Add your first investment to start tracking your portfolio

portfolio.tryThisBullet2: Compare a stock and crypto holding together
portfolio.tryThisBullet3: Review total gain/loss once you add entries
portfolio.isPortfolioRisky: Is this portfolio risky for me?
portfolio.riskReasonable: Risk looks reasonable - you're not over-concentrated in one place.
portfolio.riskModerate: Moderate risk - one area dominates; consider spreading out.
portfolio.riskHigh: Higher risk - concentration or volatility is high; worth reviewing.
portfolio.nothingMajor: Nothing major - your mix looks reasonable for a long-term approach.
portfolio.considerRebalancing: Consider rebalancing or adding another holding
portfolio.exploreMarket: Explore: Market Explorer to compare assets

insights.concentrationMeaning: One holding dominates at {{pct}}% of your portfolio.
insights.concentrationWhy: A single decline can swing your overall results.
insights.concentrationStep1: Add a second holding in a different area.
insights.diversificationMeaning: You have fewer than three holdings.
insights.diversificationWhy: Small portfolios tend to be less resilient to shocks.
insights.diversificationStep1: Add a broad market ETF or index exposure.
insights.volatilityMeaning: Crypto is about {{pct}}% of your portfolio.
insights.volatilityWhy: Crypto tends to swing more than stocks or bonds.
insights.volatilityStep1: Decide if this aligns with your risk comfort.
insights.drawdownMeaning: Your portfolio is down more than 10% overall.
insights.drawdownWhy: Large drawdowns can test your time horizon and confidence.
insights.drawdownStep1: Review your time horizon before making changes.
insights.drawdownStep2: Avoid panic selling without a plan.
insights.healthyWhy: Balanced portfolios tend to handle volatility better.
insights.healthyStep2: Explore new assets only if they fit your goal.

problems.oneInvestment: All your money is in one investment.
problems.largestHolding: Your largest holding is {{pct}}% of your portfolio - concentration is high.
problems.largestHoldingModerate: Your largest holding is {{pct}}% of your portfolio. Consider spreading risk.
problems.cryptoVolatile: Most of your portfolio is in crypto, which tends to be more volatile.
problems.downOnPaper: You're currently down on paper. Check if your timeline and risk tolerance still match this mix.
problems.spreadAcross: You're spread across more than one asset type - that helps smooth volatility.
problems.stocksPct: Stocks make up {{pct}}% of your portfolio.
problems.cryptoPct: Crypto makes up {{pct}}% - remember it can swing more than stocks.
problems.totalValueBullet: Total value is {{value}} with {{gainOrLoss}} of {{pct}}% overall.

cookieConsent.message: We use cookies on this site. We need your consent to store non-essential cookies (e.g. analytics) on your device. Necessary cookies (session, login, security) are used without consent as required to provide the service. You can accept all, reject non-essential, or choose in settings.
cookieConsent.settingsIntro: We inform you about the cookies we use. Necessary cookies (session, login, security) are always on and do not require consent under UK and EU law. Choose whether to allow optional cookies below.
cookieConsent.necessaryNote: Necessary cookies are always on (no consent required).
cookieConsent.analyticsLabel: Analytics — help us understand how the site is used (e.g. Google Analytics).
cookieConsent.marketingLabel: Marketing — optional ads or remarketing (we do not use these currently).

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: Monevo is an educational platform. This policy explains what personal data we collect, why we collect it, and your rights. It is written to comply with the UK General Data Protection Regulation (UK GDPR), the UK Data Protection Act 2018, and where applicable the EU General Data Protection Regulation (Regulation (EU) 2016/679) for users in the European Economic Area.

who.title: 1. Who we are
who.body: This policy applies to the Monevo web app and related services ("Service"). For data protection purposes, the data controller is Monevo, United Kingdom. If you are in the European Economic Area (EEA), we may act as controller for personal data processed in connection with the Service.

data.title: 2. Data we collect
data.intro: We collect the following categories of data:

account.label: Account data:
account.text: email address, username, and password hash.

profile.label: Profile and preference data:
profile.text: first/last name (if provided), onboarding responses, financial learning preferences, language, reminder settings, and theme settings.

learning.label: Learning and product usage data:
learning.text: lessons viewed, progress, streaks, missions, tool usage events, and feature interactions.

technical.label: Technical data:
technical.text: IP address, device/browser type, operating system, approximate region, timestamps, crash/error logs, and security logs.

cookies.label: Cookie and tracking data:
cookies.text: consent choices, analytics identifiers, and ad/marketing event data where consent is given.

billing.label: Billing metadata:
billing.text: plan, subscription status, and Stripe subscription identifiers. We do not store your full card number.

inputs.label: User-provided finance inputs:
inputs.text: simulated portfolio entries and learning-tool inputs entered by you.

items.direct: Directly from you (registration, onboarding, settings, tools).
items.automatic: Automatically via app logs, cookies, analytics, and pixels.
items.processors: From processors that support our service (for example Stripe for subscription status).

purposes.title: 4. Why we process your data and legal bases

core.label: Deliver core product features and learning progress

security.label: Keep the platform secure and prevent abuse

comms.label: Send service communications
comms.text: such as account and billing notices (contractual necessity and legitimate interests).

cookies.title: 5. Cookies and similar technologies
cookies.body1: We use strictly necessary cookies for login/session functions and, when you consent, analytics and marketing cookies.
cookies.body2: You can manage consent using our cookie banner and cookie settings. See our <link>Cookie Policy</link> for details.

processors.title: 6. Third-party processors and services
processors.body1: We use third parties to operate the service. Depending on your usage, these may include:

items.cookieConsent: Cookie consent (managed on our site; we do not use third-party consent tools).
items.google: Google Analytics / Google Ads tags (with consent).

processors.body2: These providers process data under contracts and only for approved purposes.

transfers.title: 7. International transfers
transfers.body: Some of our service providers process data outside the UK or the European Economic Area (EEA). Where we do so, we rely on appropriate safeguards: for example UK adequacy regulations, EU adequacy decisions, or standard contractual clauses approved by the UK Secretary of State or the European Commission.

retention.title: 8. Data retention
retention.body: We keep personal data only as long as needed for service delivery, legal obligations, dispute resolution, and security. Retention periods vary by data type.

rights.title: 9. Your rights
rights.body: Subject to applicable law (including UK GDPR and, for EEA users, EU GDPR), you may request to:

items.restrict: Restrict or object to certain processing.
items.withdraw: Withdraw consent (where processing is based on consent).
items.portable: Receive a portable copy of data you provided.
items.complaint: Lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority: in the UK, the Information Commissioner's Office (ICO); in the EEA, the data protection authority of your country of residence or place of work.

security.title: 10. Security
security.body: We use technical and organizational safeguards designed to protect personal data. No method of storage or transmission is completely secure.

children.title: 11. Children
children.body: Monevo is not intended for children under 16. If you believe a child has provided personal data, contact us and we will investigate.

changes.title: 12. Changes to this policy
changes.body: We may update this policy from time to time. Material changes will be posted in the app or on our website with an updated date.

financialDisclaimer.title: Financial Disclaimer
financialDisclaimer.intro: Monevo content is for educational and informational purposes only. This disclaimer applies to users in the United Kingdom and the European Union / European Economic Area.

items.general: Content is general information and not tailored to your personal circumstances.
items.noProfile: We do not consider your full financial situation, risk tolerance, time horizon, or investment objectives.
items.noRecommendation: Nothing on Monevo is a recommendation or solicitation to buy, sell, hold, or invest in any asset.
items.examples: Examples, scenarios, or tool outputs are illustrative and may use assumptions that do not match real-world outcomes.
items.pastPerformance: Past performance, historical data, and model outputs do not guarantee future results.

responsibility.title: Your responsibility
responsibility.body: You remain fully responsible for your own financial and investment decisions. Before acting, consider getting advice from a qualified, regulated professional.

warranty.title: No warranty on accuracy or completeness
warranty.body: We aim to provide useful and current educational content, but we do not guarantee that all content is complete, accurate, or up to date at all times.

related.title: Related notices
related.body: Please also review our <terms>Terms of Service</terms> and <privacy>Privacy Policy</privacy>.

cookiePolicy.title: Cookie Policy
cookiePolicy.intro: This policy explains what cookies and similar technologies we use and how you can manage your preferences. It is written to comply with UK rules (including the Privacy and Electronic Communications Regulations 2003 as amended, and UK GDPR where personal data is processed) and with EU rules on cookies and similar technologies (the ePrivacy Directive and the General Data Protection Regulation where applicable).

what.title: 1. What cookies are
what.body: Cookies are small text files placed on your device when you visit a website. They help with login, security, analytics, and service performance.

sessionPersistent.title: 2. Session and persistent cookies
sessionPersistent.sessionBody: Session cookies let the site recognise and link your actions during a single browsing session. They are typically used for purposes such as remembering what you put in a basket while you browse. Session cookies often store an identifier so you can move from page to page without re-entering your username, password, etc. They last only for that session and are deleted when you close the browser or app.
sessionPersistent.persistentBody: Persistent cookies are stored across sessions and can remember your preferences or actions on a site (or across sites)—for example language, theme, menu preferences, or favourite pages. We use them only where you have consented or where they are strictly necessary for the service you requested.

necessary.label: Strictly necessary cookies:
necessary.text: required for core functionality such as login, session handling, and security.

analytics.label: Analytics cookies:
analytics.text: help us understand usage and improve product experience.

marketing.label: Marketing cookies:
marketing.text: measure campaign performance and ad interactions, where consent is provided.

consentRequirement.title: 4. Consent for cookies
consentRequirement.body: In the UK, the Privacy and Electronic Communications Regulations (PECR) require that we inform you about cookies and, except for those strictly necessary for a service you have requested (e.g. session and login cookies, security), obtain your consent before storing or accessing information on your device. In the EEA, the ePrivacy rules and national implementations impose similar requirements. Consent must be informed, specific, and freely given. We do not set non-essential cookies until you have given your consent.

consent.title: 5. Managing your consent
consent.body: You can change your cookie preferences at any time via the cookie banner or your browser settings. Disabling some cookies may impact certain features.

thirdParty.title: 6. Third-party technologies
thirdParty.body: We may use third-party tools such as Google Analytics when you consent. These services may set their own cookies according to your choices in our cookie settings.

declaration.title: 7. Cookie declaration
declaration.analytics: Analytics: e.g. Google Analytics (optional, with your consent).
declaration.marketing: Marketing: not used at present; optional if we add it later.

contact.title: 8. Contact
contact.related: Related pages: <privacy>Privacy Policy</privacy> and <terms>Terms of Service</terms>

agreement.title: 1. Agreement to these terms
agreement.body: By creating an account, accessing, or using Monevo, you agree to these Terms, our <privacy>Privacy Policy</privacy>, and our <cookie>Cookie Policy</cookie>.

about.title: 2. What Monevo is (and is not)
about.body1: Monevo is a financial education and decision-support platform. Monevo is not a financial adviser, investment adviser, broker, or fiduciary.

security.title: 3. Eligibility and account security
security.body: You must provide accurate account information and keep your login credentials secure. You are responsible for activity under your account.

items.unlawful: Use the service for unlawful purposes.
items.unauthorized: Attempt unauthorized access to systems or accounts.
items.interfere: Interfere with service availability or security controls.
items.reverse: Reverse engineer or abuse platform features.

educational.title: 5. Educational content and user decisions
educational.body: Content is provided for general educational purposes only and may be incomplete, simplified, delayed, or out of date. You are solely responsible for your financial decisions and outcomes.

subscriptions.title: 6. Subscriptions, billing, and payments
subscriptions.body: Paid plans, billing intervals, and included features are displayed on the subscriptions page. Payments are processed securely by Stripe. Subscriptions renew automatically until you cancel. You may cancel at any time before the next billing date; access continues until the end of the current period. Refunds are given only where required by applicable law (for example UK Consumer Rights Act 2015).

thirdParty.title: 7. Third-party services and links
thirdParty.body: Monevo may integrate with or link to third-party services. We are not responsible for third-party content, accuracy, availability, or practices.

ip.title: 8. Intellectual property
ip.body: The platform, branding, and original content are owned by Monevo or licensors and are protected by applicable intellectual property laws. You receive a limited, non-exclusive, revocable license to use the service for personal lawful use.

disclaimers.title: 9. Disclaimers
disclaimers.body: The service is provided "as is" and "as available" without warranties of any kind, express or implied, including fitness for a particular purpose, uninterrupted availability, or error-free operation.

liability.title: 10. Limitation of liability
liability.body: To the maximum extent permitted by law, Monevo and its affiliates are not liable for indirect, incidental, consequential, special, or punitive damages, or for loss of profits, revenue, data, investment gains, or business opportunities arising from use of the service.

indemnity.title: 11. Indemnity
indemnity.body: You agree to indemnify and hold harmless Monevo from claims, losses, liabilities, and expenses arising out of your misuse of the service or breach of these terms.

termination.title: 12. Suspension and termination
termination.body: We may suspend or terminate access if we reasonably believe you have breached these terms, created security risk, or used the service unlawfully.

changes.title: 13. Changes to service and terms
changes.body: We may update product features and these terms from time to time. Continued use after updates means you accept the revised terms.

law.title: 14. Governing law and jurisdiction
law.body: These terms are governed by the laws of England and Wales. The courts of England and Wales will have non-exclusive jurisdiction, except where mandatory consumer law in your country of residence gives you the right to bring proceedings in your local courts. Nothing in these terms affects your statutory rights as a consumer: in the UK, under the Consumer Rights Act 2015 and related legislation; in the European Union and EEA, under the Consumer Rights Directive and applicable national laws.

noAdvice.title: No Financial Advice Notice
noAdvice.intro: Monevo does not provide financial, investment, tax, or legal advice. This notice applies to users in the United Kingdom and the European Union / European Economic Area.

items.position: Monevo is positioned as a financial education platform and information tool.
items.fca: In the UK: Monevo is not authorised by the Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) to provide regulated investment advice.
items.euRegulation: In the EU/EEA: Monevo does not provide investment advice as defined under Directive 2014/65/EU (MiFID II) or equivalent national law.
items.noAdvisor: Monevo does not act as your financial adviser, investment adviser, broker, or fiduciary.
items.noRelationship: Use of Monevo does not create an adviser-client or fiduciary relationship.

items.outputs: Tool outputs, rankings, prompts, and scenarios are not personal recommendations.
items.workflows: Educational workflows are not instructions to enter any trade or investment.
items.labels: Labels such as "next step" refer to learning flow, not regulated advice.

professional.title: Professional advice
professional.body: If you need advice tailored to your circumstances, speak to a qualified and appropriately regulated adviser before making financial decisions.

related.title: Related documents
related.body: Review the <financial>Financial Disclaimer</financial>, <terms>Terms of Service</terms>, and <privacy>Privacy Policy</privacy>.

owl.celebrate_0: Great job! You got it right.
